original_hypothesis.title: AeroAdaptor ignores the user-supplied `path` and always swaps tokenIn->tokenOut, enabling token routing confusion and stuck funds in adaptor
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: AeroAdaptor.swapExactTokensForTokens accepts a `path` parameter but ignores it, always constructing a single Aerodrome route using stored tokenIn/tokenOut/factory. If integrated as a generic IRouter, callers may believe they are swapping along the provided path, but the adaptor will attempt a different swap. Additionally, the adaptor pulls tokenIn from msg.sender into itself but never forwards tokenIn to the Aerodrome router explicitly; it relies on the Aerodrome router pulling from the adaptor via allowance. This can lead to stuck balances if swaps revert after transferFrom or if tokenIn/tokenOut mismatch the caller's intent.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is an integration-footgun that can cause loss of funds by misrouting swaps or sticking tokenIn in the adaptor contract. While attackers can’t generally steal others’ funds via this alone, real users/protocol components can be harmed if this is used as a drop-in UniswapV2 router.
deployment_assumptions: AeroAdaptor is wired as the router for AgentTax/BondingTax (router_ points to adaptor) or other modules that expect `path` to be respected
tokenIn/tokenOut stored in adaptor do not match the module’s expected taxToken/assetToken pair
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Deployment script v2/deployAeroAdaptor.ts deploys AeroAdaptor with env TAX_TOKEN/ASSET_TOKEN, but no evidence shown that AgentTax/BondingTax router_ is set to that adaptor on all deployments.

inventory_refs.function: swapExactTokensForTokens(uint amountIn,uint amountOutMin,address[] calldata path,address to,uint deadline)

proof_of_signal.pattern: path parameter unused; route always tokenIn->tokenOut; tokenIn pulled into adaptor before swap call

preconditions: Protocol uses AeroAdaptor as IRouter implementation for swaps
Caller/module supplies a path expecting semantics similar to UniswapV2Router
tokenIn/tokenOut configured in adaptor differ from expected path[0]/path[1]

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: interface mismatch: ignores path and hardcodes route
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: misrouting swaps, revert after funds are transferred into adaptor, and potential stuck funds
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No require(path matches tokenIn/tokenOut); no rescue function for stuck tokens

analysis_questions: Which contracts set router to AeroAdaptor (AgentTax/BondingTax) in actual deployments?
Can swaps revert after tokenIn is transferred to adaptor (e.g., deadline/amountOutMin), leaving funds stranded?
Is there any admin sweep function on AeroAdaptor? (none visible)
validation_requirements: Integration test: call swapExactTokensForTokens with mismatched path; verify behavior
Force swap revert after transferFrom to confirm tokenIn remains in adaptor
Check deployment config for router_ addresses

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Misconfiguration causes tax swaps to route incorrectly or fail after pulling taxToken into the adaptor; funds accumulate stuck in AeroAdaptor with no withdrawal path.
prerequisites: Misconfiguration or incorrect assumptions about path semantics

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Medium: stuck protocol funds (taxToken) or failed swaps leading to operational failure
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: Protocol treasury/creators (via tax distribution) rather than direct end-users
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Low if no token rescue method exists on AeroAdaptor

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing integration tests verifying adaptor complies with IRouter path semantics and failure modes

technical_investigation_focus.capital_requirements: None (can be tested with small amounts)
technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: On-chain: token balances accumulating in AeroAdaptor address
